summary: Added the reusable LLM client contract, OpenAI-compatible explicit-configuration adapter, safe JSON extraction/Zod validation/one-repair utility, fake-transport tests, and README documentation for the LLM JSON boundary slice.

tests_run.result: Passed: 10 tests across 4 suites, including valid JSON, fenced JSON extraction, parse repair success, validation/repair failure, missing config, and injected OpenAI-compatible fake fetch request shape.
Passed: Next.js production build completed. Build emitted existing @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings but exited successfully.

decisions: Kept LLM configuration explicit at the adapter boundary; no process.env reads or secret loading were added.
Made LLM completion and JSON parsing failures structured result unions so malformed assistant text does not crash callers.
Accepted only top-level object/array JSON or one whole fenced JSON block to avoid treating prose-wrapped output as valid JSON.
Limited repair to one follow-up completion using the same injected LLM client and caller-provided schema description when available.
Used fake LLM clients and injected fake fetch in tests; no network calls or real provider credentials are required.
risks: The OpenAI-compatible adapter is intentionally unconnected to runtime env wiring; later tasks must supply configuration from an approved server-side boundary.
Zod schemas are not serializable at runtime, so callers should pass schemaDescription when they want stronger repair prompts.
Next build still reports the pre-existing SWC Windows binary warning despite successful exit.
requested_action: Review the LLM service boundary, JSON extraction strictness, one-step repair behavior, and explicit configuration policy. Security review remains required by the task before acceptance.
